title: Clamp a value into a range
prompt_md:
Never trust a client-supplied number. A request might ask for `page_size=10000000`. Write `clamp(n, lo, hi)` that forces `n` into the inclusive range `[lo, hi]`:

- if `n < lo`, return `lo`
- if `n > hi`, return `hi`
- otherwise return `n` unchanged

Assume `lo <= hi`.
hints: You can do it in one line with nested `max`/`min`.
`max(lo, min(n, hi))` pins `n` to both ends at once.
reference_solution:
def clamp(n, lo, hi):
    return max(lo, min(n, hi))
